title: Steiropteris polypodioides
taxonomy: Steiropteris polypodioides is classified as a species of fern in the family Dipteridaceae. It belongs to the class Polypodiopsida, which is a group of vascular plants commonly known as true ferns. The class Polypodiopsida is further divided into several orders, with Steiropteris polypodioides belonging to the order Gleicheniales. This order is part of the division Pteridophyta, which includes all ferns and other related vascular plants. The division Pteridophyta is one of the main divisions of the kingdom Plantae.

intro: Steiropteris polypodioides, commonly known as the fern-like plant, is a type of pteridophyte, a group of vascular plants that reproduce via spores. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine due to its reported health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal properties, which can aid in the treatment of various ailments such as wounds, skin infections, and respiratory issues. Bioactive constituents of Steiropteris polypodioides include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Steiropteris polypodioides is often used to prepare decoctions, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health conditions.
